description: Silently ignoring an unrecognized flag instead of erroring, so a mistyped option makes a command run with unintended defaults.
body: Failure mode: ad-hoc argument handling drops any flag it does not recognize, so a typo like --dry-runn is ignored and the command runs for real with default (often destructive) behavior. Detection signals: a misspelled flag has no effect but the command proceeds; users report a safety flag 'not working'; no usage error for bad options. Do not ignore unknown flags. Safer fallback: use a real argument parser that errors on unrecognized options with a usage message and exit code 2, and add a test that an unknown flag fails rather than silently running.
